en.extract:
The Sunda owlet (Taenioptynx sylvaticus) is a small owl from Borneo and Sumatra.
This species was formerly treated as a subspecies of the collared owlet (Taenioptynx brodiei). It was promoted to a separate species based largely on the basis of a difference in vocalisation.
There are two subspecies:

T. s. sylvaticus (Bonaparte, 1850) - montane Sumatra
T. s. borneensis (Sharpe, 1893) - montane Borneo


References


Further reading

The '''Sunda owlet''' ('''''Taenioptynx sylvaticus''''') is a small [[typical owl|owl]] from [[Borneo]] and [[Sumatra]].

This species was formerly treated as a [[subspecies]] of the [[collared owlet]] (''Taenioptynx brodiei''). It was promoted to a separate species based largely on the basis of a difference in vocalisation.<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela Rasmussen | year=2020 | title=Owls | work=IOC World Bird List Version 10.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/owls/
 | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | accessdate=22 February 2020 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Gwee | first1=C.Y. | last2=Eaton | first2=J.A. | last3=Ng | first3=E.Y.X. | last4=Rheindt | first4=F.E. | year=2019 | title=Species delimitation within the ''Glaucidium brodiei'' owlet complex using bioacoustic tools | journal=Avian Research | volume=10 | issue=36 | doi=10.1186/s40657-019-0175-4 | doi-access=free }}</ref>

There are two [[subspecies]]:<ref>{{Cite web |title=Owls – IOC World Bird List |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/owls/ |access-date=2023-01-28 |website=www.worldbirdnames.org}}</ref>

* ''T. s. sylvaticus'' ([[Charles Lucien Bonaparte|Bonaparte]], 1850) - montane [[Sumatra]]
* ''T. s. borneensis'' ([[Richard Bowdler Sharpe|Sharpe]], 1893) - montane [[Borneo]]

==References==
{{Reflist}}

{{Refend}}

zh.extract:
巽他鸺鹠（学名：Taenioptynx sylvaticus），是鸮形目鸱鸮科的一种鸟类。


特征
巽他鸺鹠体重约59.4克，翼长约92.6毫米，嘴峰长约20.5毫米，喙宽度约6.7毫米，喙厚度约10.6毫米，跗蹠长约16.7毫米，尾长约50.4毫米。巽他鸺鹠在其分布区内为留鸟，其栖息在密集的高大树木组成的森林中，食性为肉食性，主要食物来源是陆生脊椎动物。


亚种
T. s. sylvaticus：分布于苏门答腊岛。
T. s. borneensis：分布于婆罗洲。


参考资料
